mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-run trend rate of 2.0% directly boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days without requiring additional regime filters.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of above-trend GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points compresses equity valuation multiples by raising the discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rate hikes, or the cross-sectional IC of rate changes falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth exceeding 150,000 and an unemployment rate below 4.0% signals a labor market tightness that sustains consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerating above its 3-month moving average directly increases corporate capacity utilization and margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns turn negative when INDPRO accelerates during late-cycle phases, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when GDP growth decelerates below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: New housing starts falling below 1.10 million annualized units signals a collapse in construction demand and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of months where housing starts drop below 1.10 million, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales revenue combined with accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and squeezes profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the cross-sectional rank correlation between the interaction term and forward returns exceeds 0.05 in the wrong direction.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 consistently supports aggregate demand and corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days regardless of broader economic conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in high-employment-growth months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI index exceeding 0.5% signals unanchored inflation expectations that force aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of months where CPI accelerates above 0.5%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when real yields remain negative.
